name: Thai Green Curry Chicken with Basil
recipeIngredient: 3 cans Unsweetened coconut milk (do not shake)
2 Whole chicken breasts, Boned, skinned, cut into 1-inch cubes
8 Dried or fresh kaffir lime leaves, or fresh citrus leaves, washed
3/4 cup Basil leaves (Thai basil or Standard green basil)
2 cups Fresh green peas OR 8 small Thai eggplants
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the green curry paste by combining all ingredients in a bowl until well mixed.
- You can adjust the heat level by adding more peppers as desired.
2. Cook the chicken cubes in a wok or saucepan with 3/4 cup of coconut milk, bringing to a boil and simmering for about 5 minutes until tender.
3. In another pot, add the galangal and fish sauce, cooking over high heat until thick and oily.
4. Stir in the green curry paste and cook for about 2 minutes until aromatic.
5. Add the chicken and citrus leaves, stirring frequently over medium heat for an additional 2 minutes.
6. Pour in the remaining coconut milk, basil leaves, chilies, and peas, bringing to a boil and simmering for another 5 minutes.
7. Adjust the sauce consistency with chicken stock or water if needed.
8. Garnish with the remaining basil leaves and serve hot over rice.
